# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

# Start with a base image, e.g. latest Python on Alpine Linux
FROM python:alpine

# Make directories for app, logs and temporary files
RUN mkdir -p /app /data/logs /data/cache

# Set directory permissions
RUN chmod 777 /data/logs /data/cache

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy all files from the current directory to /app in the container
COPY . .

# Allow statements and log messages to be immediately dumped to the stream
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED 1

# Expose ports for communication
EXPOSE 8000

# Run the application
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
